content:
Hi, as discussed during the last Rust DRM call, we should be doing a first pass on abstractions we intend to tentatively "fast track" under the new EXPERIMENTAL Kconfig.

https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/merge_requests/14

Here is the GPUVM code, with a few minor touch ups from me.

This link is to a Gitlab MR for the sole reason of allowing us to use the Gitlab UI to make this process easier. The panfrost repo is just the first remote I could think of to allow all of us to collaborate, since I don't think I have the rights to push anywhere else for now.

Note that this code has been tested and is known to be working on both Asahi and Tyr.
cc @Danilo Krummrich @Simona Vetter @Lyude @Dave Airlie @Alice Ryhl @Alyssa Rosenzweig @Philipp Stanner @Rob Herring @Janne Grunau
As for basing this on top of staging/nova-drm, I have a question: what tree has the latest drm bindings? Apparently that is not rust-next

thanks. I think staging/nova-drm has the most recent version drm bindings. I imported those for asahi when I rebased onto Danilo's PCI and platform abstractions and we have no notable additional changes.
I'm currently cleaning up the the asahi tree to get a view what missing for upstreaming. Still dealing with replacing old github rust-for-linux imports with changes from upstream or recent patches and dropping unnecessary changes.

I think staging/nova-drm has the most recent version drm bindings.

Currently yes, but generally I was storing the latest version in drm-misc/topic/rust-drm.

I also plan to pick up the DRM abstraction work this week again, i.e. rebase it, add some improvements / fixes, and sketch up the proposal regarding the drvdata issue we've been discussing.

Janne Grunau said:

Still dealing with replacing old github rust-for-linux imports with changes from upstream or recent patches and dropping unnecessary changes.

Do you require any help here? I'd be willing to give a hand, if needed.

@Janne Grunau fyi, I've been working on fences with @Alice Ryhl . We plan to offer both the C abstraction but also a new, Rust-based version that should hopefully work better with the rust-native scheduler planned by Danilo and Philip.
(may I move this thread to DRM channel?)
@Daniel Almeida We don't know where we're heading with the GPU scheduler yet. This is just one option we may consider. :smile:
This topic was moved here from #General > Asahi GPUVM review thread by Danilo Krummrich.

Daniel Almeida said:

Janne Grunau fyi, I've been working on fences with Alice Ryhl . We plan to offer both the C abstraction but also a new, Rust-based version that should hopefully work better with the rust-native scheduler planned by Danilo and Philip.

If this is about dma_fence then I'm really confused how you can do a rust-based version of them. Because they're cross-driver objects that can be shared, mutual interop with C fences is required for pretty much everything (some driver internal things you might be able to roll your own, right until you need to use gpuvm or ttm or something like that).

Abstraction looks plausible to me but, I don't have enough experience with either Rust or DRM to be able to review bindings sensibly. (I'm comfortable enough with driver code written in safe Rust and/or using DRM helpers. That's very different from understanding the subtle locking reqs for unsafe Rust in binding code etc)

Simona Vetter said:

Daniel Almeida said:

Janne Grunau fyi, I've been working on fences with Alice Ryhl . We plan to offer both the C abstraction but also a new, Rust-based version that should hopefully work better with the rust-native scheduler planned by Danilo and Philip.

If this is about dma_fence then I'm really confused how you can do a rust-based version of them. Because they're cross-driver objects that can be shared, mutual interop with C fences is required for pretty much everything (some driver internal things you might be able to roll your own, right until you need to use gpuvm or ttm or something like that).

Hi Sima, indeed I didn't consider what you said, sorry for the confusion
